title: Trivy scan
description: Scans the workspace for known vulnerabilities and for committed secrets.

scanners.description: What to look for. `misconfig` is deliberately NOT a default: this repo has no Dockerfile, no Kubernetes and no Terraform, so it would scan nothing and report success — a green check that means nothing is worse than no check at all.

severity.description: Severities that fail the build. Below HIGH the noise drowns the signal and people stop reading it.

ignoreUnfixed.description: Skip vulnerabilities with no fix available: there is nothing to do about them, and a report you cannot act on teaches you to stop reading it.

skipDirs.description: Not scanned. node_modules is already covered through the lockfile, which is the source of truth; the rest is build output.

runner.description: How to run Trivy. `auto` prefers the binary on PATH and falls back to Docker. `docker` FORCES the pinned image — that is what CI uses, so the result never depends on whichever trivy happens to be installed on a runner.

includeDevDeps.description: Scan devDependencies too. ON by default, and that is not Trivy's default: it suppresses them. In THIS workspace almost everything is a devDependency — the runtime deps are React and React Router — so leaving them out would mean scanning almost nothing and reporting a confident zero.

project.description: Scan what THIS app ships instead of the workspace. Nx's project graph is asked for the app's production dependencies, and a package.json + pruned lockfile are written for exactly those. Without it the scan reads the workspace lockfile — which is the toolbox (Playwright, Vitest, ESLint), not the app.
